info_gaps: current INR (last value 2.8 from prior week)
any focal neuro deficit on exam

resource.note: only 1 monitored bed left — likely needed for observation ≥6h

resource.neurosurgery_on_call: not listed; neurology ETA 25 min, surgery ETA 20 min

warnings: Warfarin + head trauma + brief LOC = high risk intracranial hemorrhage even with GCS 15
Last INR 2.8 (therapeutic-high); current INR may be higher
Consider reversal agents (4F-PCC, vitamin K) on standby pending CT and INR
Observation ≥6h required even if initial CT negative

hypotheses.name: Uncomplicated minor head injury / scalp hematoma
rules_against: anticoagulation alone mandates CT regardless of GCS
elderly faller has high baseline ICH risk

hypotheses.name: Cervical spine injury from same fall
Syncope/arrhythmia (AFib) as cause of fall
rules_against: no chest pain or palpitations reported

immediate.reason: Head trauma on warfarin mandates immediate CT regardless of GCS.

immediate.action: STAT INR/PT, CBC, BMP, type & screen; place 2 large-bore IVs
immediate.reason: Quantify anticoagulation level and prepare for possible reversal (4F-PCC + vitamin K) if ICH.

immediate.action: Place on cardiac monitor in monitored bed; neuro checks q15min
immediate.reason: AFib + LOC + anticoagulated head injury — need continuous monitoring and serial neuro exams.

monitor.action: Serial GCS and focal neuro exam q15min until CT result, then per disposition
monitor.reason: Detect delayed deterioration; warfarin-related ICH can evolve slowly.

monitor.action: Plan ≥6h observation with repeat CT or extended obs even if first CT negative
monitor.reason: Anticoagulated head trauma carries delayed bleed risk per ACEP policy.

escalate_or_redirect.action: Notify neurosurgery on standby; have 4F-PCC + vitamin K 10mg IV ready pending CT/INR
escalate_or_redirect.reason: If CT shows ICH, reversal must start within minutes — pre-stage now.

explanation.summary: 67M on warfarin (last INR 2.8) with witnessed fall, head strike, brief LOC, occipital hematoma — GCS 15 and vitals stable but anticoagulated head trauma is a tier-2 high-risk presentation. Mandatory STAT CT head, INR, and observation ≥6h; reversal agents pre-staged. Tier 'now' chosen conservatively despite normal vitals.

citations.signal: head injury on warfarin → CT STAT + INR + ≥6h obs regardless of GCS
high-risk situation (anticoagulated TBI with LOC) → ESI 2

checklist.task: Order and complete CT head non-contrast STAT
Draw STAT INR/PT, CBC, BMP, type & screen
Place patient in monitored bed with cardiac telemetry
Pre-stage 4F-PCC and vitamin K 10mg IV from pharmacy
Confirm last warfarin dose timing and clarify mechanism/LOC duration with patient/family
Reassess for c-spine tenderness and clear or collar
Disposition decision (admit obs vs neurosurgery) after CT + INR

why_this_tier: R-HEAD-ANTICOAG-01 explicitly classifies warfarin + any head injury as ESI 2 regardless of GCS
Documented brief LOC raises ICH pre-test probability
Last INR 2.8 (upper therapeutic) — bleeding risk meaningful
Conservative bias: occult ICH on anticoagulants can decompensate without warning
would_downgrade_if: Patient not actually on anticoagulation (med reconciliation error)
No LOC, no head strike on re-history, no scalp finding
CT head negative AND INR <1.5 AND 6h obs uneventful — then downgrade disposition, not initial tier
would_upgrade_if: Vomiting, severe headache, or new hypertension/bradycardia (Cushing)
INR significantly supratherapeutic (>3.5) or CT shows hemorrhage
